# WCPOS 1.8.15

## What's new

### Receipt Data v1 contract for custom templates
Receipt templates now have a stronger v1 data contract with branchable tax display fields, a tax-summary guard, richer i18n labels, fiscal/refund context, and expanded store/customer/line metadata. This gives custom templates more room to grow without relying on hidden renderer-only helpers.

### Redesigned template gallery and editor previews
The receipt gallery and template editor now include refreshed A4, invoice, quote, packing slip, gift receipt, detailed thermal, simple thermal, and RTL templates. Preview rendering is shared across gallery/editor surfaces, uses scaled paper viewports, and handles thermal/logicless previews more consistently.

### Tax-inclusive receipt correctness
Receipts can now adapt wording for tax-inclusive and tax-exclusive stores. Tax summaries also use real post-discount taxable bases instead of reverse-calculating from rounded tax amounts, avoiding cent-level drift on inclusive-price orders.

### Store sync validation fix
Removed the vestigial `order_barcode_type` field from store data so desktop app store documents are not rejected by strict schemas.

### Discount and coupon cleanup
Coupon discounts now emit one row per coupon. `discounts[].code` is the canonical coupon reference, and `discounts[].label` uses a distinct coupon description when available instead of duplicating the code.

### Store details and tax IDs
Store Details and Tax IDs were consolidated in settings, tax ID schema handling was aligned, and compatibility safeguards were added for older Pro overrides.

### CI and contract safeguards
Added receipt field-tree/payload coverage, receipt schema checks, safer raw template-content guards, and stricter merge-gate handling to catch contract drift earlier.

## References
